title: Etched - Building AI Hardware to Make Inference Faster and Cheaper - [Invest Like the Best, EP.480]
summary:
## TL;DR
Gavin Uberti and Rob Lockett, founders of Edged, discuss their journey in building a better AI chip, taping out a working chip on their first attempt, and becoming the first hardware company founded after ChatGPT to do so. They share their technical bets, architecture approaches, and the process of deciding what technologies to invent. The conversation also explores the importance of inference, the current state of the industry, and the need for new hardware to serve giant models at scale.

## Key Themes
* The founders' personal stories and motivations for starting Edged, including Rob's experience with cancer and Gavin's early start in kernel development
* The technical challenges and opportunities in building a new AI chip, including the importance of low voltage inference and cluster-scale memory
* The current state of the industry, including the limitations of existing AI chips and the need for new architectures to serve giant models at scale
* The importance of inference and its potential to become one of the largest markets in the world
* The competitive drive and innovative spirit of the founders, including their participation in high school competitions and their desire to push the boundaries of what is possible

## VC Signals & Market Moves
* Edged has raised $800 million to build its first product and has over $1 billion in customer demand
* The company is building a full inference solution, including a chip, power delivery, board, interconnect, and production for mass volume of racks
* Gavin and Rob believe that inference will become one of the largest markets in the world, with a potential for giant models to serve billions of users
* The founders are taking a unique approach to building their AI chip, including the use of low voltage inference and cluster-scale memory

## Notable Quotes
* "There's a certain level of naivety required to think that you could build a chip better than every other AI chip ever built and build a company to do it way faster than ever has been done." — Gavin Uberti
* "We're not just building a chip. We're building a full inference solution, and that means a rack. That means the chip. That means the power delivery into the chip." — Rob Lockett
* "If we can take an agent that can run at a certain model quality and could take a year to solve a certain task using inference time compute, if you have way faster decode speed, you can compress that into a month." — Gavin Uberti

## Vocabulary & Concepts to Know
* Denard scaling: the concept that voltage is quadratically proportional to power
* Low voltage inference: a new mechanism of running at much lower voltages, a new type of power delivery
* Cluster-scale memory: a technology that allows for way more bandwidth and a much lower latency from chip to chip to interconnects
* MFU (Model Flops Utilization): a concept that measures the actual flops utilized when running real workloads
* KV cache: a type of memory that stores the model's memories, or "knowledge"
* HPM (High-Performance Memory): a type of memory used in some AI chips
* SRAM (Static Random-Access Memory): a type of memory used in some AI chips
* 3D DRAM: a type of memory that stacks memory dies on top of compute dies
* EDA tools: Electronic Design Automation tools used to design and simulate chips
* Interconnects: the connections between chips that allow them to communicate with each other
